game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Cut the Rope: Time Travel GOLD:** Players solve physics-based puzzles by interacting with various objects to guide a piece of candy to two hungry monsters, Om Nom and his ancestor, within each level. The primary control involves swiping a finger across ropes to cut them, causing the candy to fall or swing, while other interactive elements like air cushions, bubbles, and time-slowing devices are activated by tapping them. Each level requires both monsters to consume the candy to progress, with optional stars collected by having the candy pass through them before reaching the monsters, contributing to a 3-star rating per level. Progression occurs across distinct historical era packs, such as the Middle Ages or Ancient Egypt, each introducing new environmental mechanics and increasing puzzle complexity.
game_info.description: Guide the candy to feed both Om Nom and his ancestor by cutting ropes and activating physics-based mechanisms. Each level features suspended candy that must reach both monsters to complete the stage. Collect all three stars along the way for a perfect score. Navigate through historical eras with unique challenges - bubbles that float candy upward, air cushions that bounce objects, and gravity zones that alter physics. Strategic timing and rope cutting are essential to success!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys (←/→): Select which rope to cut or which device to activate
Space: Cut the selected rope or activate the selected device
R: Restart level (during gameplay)
Enter: Start game / Continue to next level
Esc: Pause / Unpause game

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic rope cutting mechanics and candy physics. Verifies that cutting ropes causes the candy to fall naturally and that the physics simulation responds correctly to gravity and swinging motion.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Automatically cut the first rope after 1 second, then cut the second rope after 2 seconds. Monitor the candy's position and velocity to ensure it falls and swings realistically according to Matter.js physics.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The candy should swing and fall when ropes are cut, demonstrating proper physics behavior. The test succeeds if the candy moves downward with realistic velocity and the game doesn't crash during rope cutting operations.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing complete level win condition by ensuring candy reaches both monsters. Verifies collision detection between candy and monsters, star collection mechanics, and proper transition to win state.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Automatically cut ropes in the optimal sequence to guide candy through all stars and into both monsters. Use precise timing to ensure the candy collects stars before reaching each monster.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Both monsters should eat the candy, all three stars should be collected, and the game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN state. Success is determined by gamePhase becoming "GAME_OVER_WIN" and score reflecting collected stars.
